Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She had a big, red ball. Lily loved to play with her ball every day. One day, she saw a boy named Tom. Tom was sad because he had no toys to play with.
Lily wanted to help Tom. She thought of a plan. She would give Tom a toy to play with. Lily went to Tom and said, "I have a toy for you. Let's play together!" Tom was very happy.
Lily and Tom played with the big, red ball. They had lots of fun. Tom was not sad anymore. They became good friends and played together every day. And they lived happily ever after.

Lily had an idea. She took a small piece of paper and gave it to the bird. The bird flew down and started to fold the paper into a beautiful shape. Lily was happy to see the bird fly again.
Lily showed the bird the folded paper. The bird looked at the paper and then at the bird. The bird was now a beautiful butterfly! Lily felt proud of her work. She showed the bird to her mom and dad. They loved it and knew that Lily had done a good thing.

One day, a brave girl named Mia went to the beach. She saw a big surf. Mia was very happy. She wanted to play on the surf. She asked her friend Tom to come with her.
"Let's play on the surf!" said Tom. Mia was excited. They played on the surf all day. They had lots of fun.
But then, a big wave came. The wave was very strong. It pushed Mia and Tom off the surf. They tried to hold on, but the waves were too strong. Mia and Tom were sad.
"I'm sorry, Mia," said Tom. "I didn't mean to scare you."
"It's okay, Tom," said Mia. "We can still play on the beach. But next time, be more careful."
They hugged and played on the beach. They were happy again. They learned to be more careful on the surf.
